articles.headline: Kerala Declared First Indian State to Eradicate Extreme Poverty, Setting National Benchmark
articles.explainer:
• Kerala has officially achieved the eradication of extreme poverty, making it the first state in India to reach this milestone.
• The achievement is attributed to robust social welfare schemes, targeted interventions, and effective implementation of poverty alleviation programs.
• This success is a result of years of concerted efforts focusing on providing basic necessities, employment opportunities, and social security nets to vulnerable populations.
• The 'Kerala Model' of development, emphasizing public services and grassroots planning, is being studied as a potential blueprint for other states.
• The initiative included identifying and supporting families below the extreme poverty line through various government and local self-government projects.
prelimsPoints: Kerala is the first Indian state to eradicate extreme poverty.
The achievement is based on a multi-dimensional approach to poverty.
The 'Kerala Model' is often cited for its focus on social development indicators.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key components of the 'Kerala Model' that contributed to extreme poverty eradication.
Analyze the role of decentralized governance and social welfare schemes in achieving this milestone.
Examine the potential replicability of Kerala's success in other Indian states, considering socio-economic diversities.
articles.imageDescription: A map of Kerala highlighting its achievement in poverty eradication.
articles.trivia: Kerala boasts one of the highest Human Development Index (HDI) scores among Indian states, often compared to some developed nations.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches CMS-03, India's Heaviest Communications Satellite
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched CMS-03, its heaviest communications satellite to date, from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre in Andhra Pradesh.
• CMS-03 is designed to provide advanced telecommunication services, including high-speed internet, broadcasting, and disaster management support across India and its surrounding regions.
• The satellite's enhanced capacity and extended lifespan are expected to significantly boost India's communication infrastructure.
• This launch marks another major milestone for ISRO, reaffirming India's growing capabilities in space technology and applications.
• The mission utilized ISRO's Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle (GSLV) or a similar heavy-lift launcher.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology
prelimsPoints: CMS-03 is ISRO's heaviest communications satellite.
It was launched from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
It aims to enhance telecommunication services.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of advanced communication satellites like CMS-03 for India's digital infrastructure and national security.
Analyze ISRO's role in leveraging space technology for socio-economic development in India.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India's space program in the coming decade, particularly in heavy-lift capabilities.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of the CMS-03 satellite orbiting Earth.
articles.trivia: India's first experimental satellite, Aryabhata, was launched by the Soviet Union in 1975.

articles.headline: Historic Triumph: India Clinches Maiden Women's Cricket World Cup Title 2025
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Women's Cricket Team made history by winning their first-ever Women's Cricket World Cup title in 2025.
• This victory marks a significant milestone for women's sports in India, boosting morale and inspiring a new generation of athletes.
• The team's consistent performance throughout the tournament culminated in a thrilling final match, showcasing exceptional talent and teamwork.
• The win is expected to further popularize women's cricket and lead to increased investment and recognition for female athletes in the country.
• Celebrations erupted nationwide as fans lauded the team's groundbreaking achievement.
prelimsPoints: India won its first Women's Cricket World Cup in 2025.
This is a significant achievement for women's sports in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the impact of such sporting achievements on promoting gender equality and women's empowerment in India.
Analyze the role of sports in fostering national identity and unity, using the example of India's World Cup win.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for professional women's sports in India, particularly in terms of infrastructure and funding.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Women's Cricket team celebrating their World Cup victory.
articles.trivia: The first Women's Cricket World Cup was held in England in 1973, two years before the men's inaugural event.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh Court Sentences Five to Death for 2015 Chittoor Mayor Assassination
articles.explainer:
• A special court in Andhra Pradesh has sentenced five individuals to death for the assassination of Chittoor Mayor Katari Anuradha and her husband Katari Mohan in November 2015.
• The verdict comes after nearly a decade of trial, underscoring the judicial system's commitment to delivering justice in high-profile criminal cases.
• The assassination, believed to be politically motivated, had sent shockwaves across the state and raised concerns about political violence.
• The court's decision is seen as a strong message against political vigilantism and criminal elements in public life.
• The case involved extensive investigation and prosecution, highlighting the complexities of bringing perpetrators of such crimes to justice.
gsSubTopics: Structure, Organization and Functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
prelimsPoints: Five people sentenced to death for the 2015 Chittoor Mayor assassination.
The verdict was delivered by a court in Andhra Pradesh.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of the judiciary in curbing political violence and ensuring accountability in high-profile criminal cases.
Analyze the debates surrounding capital punishment in India, in light of such verdicts.
Examine the challenges faced by the Indian criminal justice system in ensuring speedy trials for complex and politically sensitive cases.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel and court files, symbolizing the judicial process.
articles.trivia: The Indian Penal Code (IPC) specifies various offenses for which capital punishment can be awarded, including murder.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi Alleges Massive Electoral Fraud in 'H Files' Press Conference
articles.explainer:
• Congress leader Rahul Gandhi conducted a press conference titled 'H files,' making serious allegations of massive electoral fraud in the 2024 Haryana Legislative Assembly election.
• Gandhi presented what he claimed to be evidence of irregularities, including manipulation of voter lists, electronic voting machine (EVM) tampering, and undue influence.
• The allegations have sparked a political controversy, with demands for an independent inquiry into the electoral process in Haryana.
• The ruling party has vehemently denied the accusations, calling them baseless and an attempt to discredit democratic institutions.
• This incident reignites the debate on electoral reforms, transparency, and the integrity of the election process in India.
prelimsPoints: Rahul Gandhi made allegations of electoral fraud in the 2024 Haryana Legislative Assembly election.
The allegations were made during a press conference titled 'H files'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges to the integrity of electoral processes in India and the measures needed to enhance transparency and public trust.
Analyze the role of the Election Commission of India in addressing allegations of electoral malpractices and ensuring free and fair elections.
Examine the arguments for and against the use of EVMs in Indian elections, considering recent controversies.
articles.imageDescription: Rahul Gandhi addressing a press conference with 'H files' banner.
articles.trivia: The first general election in independent India was held from October 1951 to February 1952.

articles.headline: Nation Gripped by Multiple Fatal Road and Rail Accidents Across Jodhpur, Jaipur, Chevella, Chhattisgarh, and Mirzapur
articles.explainer:
• A series of tragic accidents occurred across India, including a tempo traveller crashing into a parked truck in Jodhpur, killing 15.
• In Jaipur, 19 people died and 40 were injured when a speeding dump truck collided with 17 vehicles, causing a massive pile-up.
• Near Chevella, Telangana, 24 people were killed and 24 injured when a truck collided with an RTC bus.
• A passenger train collided with a cargo train near Bilaspur, Chhattisgarh, resulting in 11 fatalities in what's termed the '2025 Chhattisgarh train collision'.
• Six people were killed after being hit by the Kalka-Howrah Express while crossing railway tracks in Mirzapur, Uttar Pradesh.
• These incidents highlight critical issues in road and rail safety, infrastructure, and emergency response mechanisms in India.
prelimsPoints: Major fatal road accidents reported in Jodhpur (15 killed), Jaipur (19 killed), and Chevella (24 killed).
A passenger-cargo train collision in Bilaspur, Chhattisgarh, killed 11.
Six people died after being hit by a train in Mirzapur, UP.
mainsPoints: Analyze the major causes of road and rail accidents in India and propose comprehensive measures for enhancing safety standards.
Discuss the role of technology, infrastructure development, and public awareness campaigns in mitigating accident risks.
Evaluate the effectiveness of India's disaster management framework in responding to mass casualty events and suggest improvements.
articles.imageDescription: Rescue workers at the site of a train collision.
articles.trivia: India accounts for about 11% of the total number of road accident deaths in the world.

articles.headline: At Least Nine Killed in Stampede at Venkateswara Temple in Srikakulam
articles.explainer:
• A tragic stampede at the Venkateswara Temple in Srikakulam, Andhra Pradesh, resulted in the deaths of at least nine people.
• The incident occurred during a major religious festival, where a large crowd had gathered, leading to overcrowding and a crush.
• Several others were injured and received immediate medical attention, with authorities launching an inquiry into the cause of the stampede.
• This event highlights the persistent challenges in crowd management and safety protocols at large religious gatherings in India.
• State authorities have announced ex-gratia payments for the victims' families and initiated steps to prevent recurrence.
prelimsPoints: A stampede at Venkateswara Temple in Srikakulam, Andhra Pradesh, killed at least nine people.
The incident occurred during a religious festival.
mainsPoints:
Discuss the recurring issue of stampedes at religious sites in India and analyze the systemic failures in crowd management.
Suggest comprehensive strategies for enhancing public safety and disaster preparedness at large gatherings, especially religious festivals.
Examine the role of local administration, temple trusts, and technology in preventing such tragedies.
articles.imageDescription: Emergency services at the scene of a stampede.
articles.trivia: Major stampedes in India often occur during festivals like Kumbh Mela or at popular pilgrimage sites due to massive crowds.

articles.headline: Four United Kuki National Army Militants Killed in Manipur Encounter
articles.explainer:
• Four militants belonging to the United Kuki National Army (UKNA) were killed in an encounter with security forces in Churachandpur, Manipur.
• The operation was part of ongoing efforts by security forces to counter insurgency and maintain peace and stability in the region.
• The encounter highlights the persistent security challenges and ethnic tensions in certain parts of Manipur.
• Weapons and incriminating documents were recovered from the site of the encounter, suggesting planned militant activities.
• This incident underscores the complex internal security scenario in India's North-Eastern states.
gsSubTopics: Security Challenges and their Management in Border Areas
Linkages of Organized Crime with Terrorism
Various Security Forces and Agencies and their Mandate
prelimsPoints: Four UKNA militants were killed in an encounter in Churachandpur, Manipur.
The incident is related to ongoing efforts against insurgency in the North-East.
mainsPoints:
Analyze the root causes of insurgency in Manipur and the broader North-Eastern region of India.
Discuss the strategies adopted by the Indian government and security forces to counter militancy and bring peace to the region.
Examine the role of ethnic dynamics and cross-border influences in perpetuating security challenges in the North-East.
articles.imageDescription: Indian security forces patrolling a forested area in Manipur.
articles.trivia: Manipur shares a border with Myanmar, which often contributes to cross-border insurgency activities.

articles.headline: RBI's Monetary Policy Committee Concludes Meeting, Focus on Inflation and Growth Outlook
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its latest meeting, with key decisions expected on interest rates and liquidity measures.
• The primary focus of the MPC remains on managing inflation within the target band while supporting economic growth amidst global uncertainties.
• Experts anticipate the RBI to maintain a cautious stance, potentially holding the repo rate steady or making marginal adjustments based on current economic indicators.
• The committee's forward guidance will be crucial for market stability and investor sentiment, particularly regarding future inflation trajectory and growth projections.
• Discussions likely included global economic headwinds, domestic demand, credit growth, and the impact of monsoon on food inflation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources, Growth, Development and Employment
prelimsPoints: RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) met to review economic conditions.
Key focus areas are inflation management and economic growth.
MPC decisions influence interest rates and liquidity in the economy.
mainsPoints:
Evaluate the effectiveness of RBI's monetary policy tools in managing inflation and supporting economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Discuss the challenges faced by the MPC in balancing inflation control with the imperative for economic recovery.
Analyze the interplay between fiscal policy and monetary policy in achieving macroeconomic stability in India.
articles.imageDescription: RBI logo and graphs depicting economic indicators.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Continues Deliberations on Petitions Related to Uniform Civil Code
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India continued its deliberations on a batch of petitions challenging and supporting the implementation of a Uniform Civil Code (UCC).
• The court is examining various aspects, including constitutional validity, implications for personal laws, and the impact on religious and cultural diversity.
• The debate surrounding UCC involves fundamental rights, secularism, and the state's power to legislate on personal matters.
• Various stakeholders, including religious bodies, legal experts, and women's rights organizations, are presenting their arguments before the court.
• A potential ruling or guidance from the Supreme Court could have far-reaching consequences for family laws across different communities in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—Historical Underpinnings, Evolution, Features, Amendments, Significant Provisions and Basic Structure
Functions and Responsibilities of the Union and the States
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court is deliberating on petitions related to the Uniform Civil Code (UCC).
UCC is mentioned under Article 44 of the Indian Constitution (DPSP).
It aims to replace diverse personal laws with a common set of laws governing marriage, divorce, inheritance, etc.
mainsPoints:
Critically analyze the arguments for and against the implementation of a Uniform Civil Code in India, considering its constitutional provisions and socio-cultural implications.
Discuss how a UCC could impact gender justice and religious freedom, drawing upon various perspectives.
Examine the role of the judiciary in interpreting and guiding the implementation of sensitive policy matters like the UCC.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building.
articles.trivia: Goa is the only Indian state currently with a Uniform Civil Code, inherited from its Portuguese civil law tradition.

articles.headline: India-Africa Economic Forum Concludes, Pledges Enhanced Cooperation and New Partnerships
articles.explainer:
• The India-Africa Economic Forum concluded with commitments for enhanced cooperation across various sectors, including trade, investment, technology transfer, and capacity building.
• The forum emphasized strengthening South-South cooperation and fostering sustainable development partnerships between India and African nations.
• Several new agreements and memoranda of understanding (MoUs) were signed, particularly in areas of renewable energy, digital infrastructure, and healthcare.
• Leaders highlighted the shared vision for a multipolar world order and mutual growth, moving beyond traditional donor-recipient dynamics.
• The forum underscored India's growing strategic and economic engagement with the African continent.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, Regional and Global Groupings and Agreements involving India
Effect of Policies and Politics of Developed and Developing Countries on India’s Interests
prelimsPoints: The India-Africa Economic Forum focused on strengthening trade, investment, and technology transfer.
New agreements were signed in sectors like renewable energy and digital infrastructure.
It emphasizes South-South cooperation.
mainsPoints:
Analyze the strategic significance of India's engagement with African nations in the context of global geopolitics and economic competition.
Discuss the key pillars of India's foreign policy towards Africa and evaluate its effectiveness in fostering mutual growth and development.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in expanding India-Africa cooperation, particularly in areas of infrastructure and resource development.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and African flags displayed at a conference venue.
articles.trivia: India is one of the largest trading partners of Africa, with trade volumes continuously increasing over the past two decades.

articles.headline: Air Quality Deteriorates in Northern India, Raising Public Health Concerns
articles.explainer:
• Air quality across major cities in Northern India, including Delhi-NCR, has significantly deteriorated, entering 'very poor' and 'severe' categories.
• The worsening air quality is attributed to a combination of meteorological factors, stubble burning in neighboring states, vehicular emissions, and industrial pollution.
• Public health advisories have been issued, urging residents to take precautions, especially vulnerable groups like children and the elderly.
• This annual phenomenon highlights the urgent need for long-term, coordinated strategies to combat air pollution across state boundaries.
• Discussions are ongoing regarding emergency measures such as odd-even schemes, construction bans, and restrictions on polluting industries.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, Environmental Pollution and Degradation, Environmental Impact Assessment
prelimsPoints: Air quality in Northern India, especially Delhi-NCR, deteriorated to 'very poor'/'severe' categories.
Causes include stubble burning, vehicular emissions, and industrial pollution.
AQI measures the level of air pollution.
mainsPoints:
Analyze the multi-faceted causes of air pollution in Northern India and critically evaluate the effectiveness of current policy interventions.
Discuss the socio-economic and health impacts of deteriorating air quality and suggest comprehensive, long-term solutions involving inter-state cooperation.
Examine the role of technology, public awareness, and behavioral changes in mitigating air pollution.
articles.imageDescription: A hazy skyline of Delhi, indicating severe air pollution.
articles.trivia: PM2.5 refers to atmospheric particulate matter that has a diameter of less than 2.5 micrometers, which is about 3% the diameter of a human hair.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New National Policy on Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI)
articles.explainer:
• The Indian government unveiled a new comprehensive National Policy on Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI), aimed at accelerating digital transformation.
• The policy focuses on expanding access to digital services, promoting innovation, and ensuring data privacy and security for citizens.
• It outlines a framework for the development and governance of foundational digital systems like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, and their integration.
• The DPI policy is expected to further boost India's digital economy and enhance the delivery of public services, fostering financial inclusion and economic empowerment.
• Emphasis is placed on creating an open, secure, and interoperable digital ecosystem, leveraging India's success in digital payments and identity.
gsSubTopics: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Government launched a new National Policy on Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
DPI includes systems like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
The policy aims to expand digital services, promote innovation, and ensure data security.
mainsPoints:
Discuss the significance of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in fostering inclusive growth and efficient governance in India.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a robust DPI framework, including data privacy concerns, digital divide, and cybersecurity threats.
Evaluate how India's DPI model can serve as a blueprint for other developing nations seeking digital transformation.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting interconnected digital services and platforms.
articles.trivia: India's Unified Payments Interface (UPI) has become a global benchmark for real-time digital payments, processing billions of transactions monthly.

articles.headline: Noted Hindi Poet and Writer Ramdarash Mishra Passes Away at 101
articles.explainer:
• Renowned Hindi poet and writer Ramdarash Mishra passed away at the age of 101, leaving behind a rich legacy in Indian literature.
• Mishra was celebrated for his profound contributions to Hindi poetry, fiction, and literary criticism, spanning several decades.
• His works often reflected the rural Indian landscape, human emotions, and social realities, resonating deeply with readers.
• He was a recipient of numerous prestigious literary awards, recognizing his significant impact on contemporary Hindi literature.
• His demise marks the end of an era for Hindi literary circles, with tributes pouring in from across the country.
gsSubTopics: Indian Culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: Ramdarash Mishra, a noted Hindi poet and writer, passed away at 101.
He was known for his contributions to Hindi literature and received various awards.
articles.trivia: Ramdarash Mishra was born in Dumri village, Gorakhpur district, Uttar Pradesh.

articles.headline: Latest Phase of National Green Hydrogen Mission Launched, Boosting Renewable Energy Goals
articles.explainer:
• The government launched the latest phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, intensifying efforts to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
• This phase focuses on scaling up production capacities, developing green hydrogen export infrastructure, and fostering domestic manufacturing of electrolyzers.
• The mission aims to reduce India's reliance on fossil fuel imports and contribute significantly to its climate change commitments and energy security.
• Financial incentives, R&D support, and policy frameworks are being strengthened to attract investments and accelerate technological advancements in the sector.
• Green hydrogen is seen as a critical component in decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors like steel, refining, and fertilizers.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, Environmental Pollution and Degradation, Environmental Impact Assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Latest phase of National Green Hydrogen Mission launched.
Aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen.
Focuses on scaling production, export infrastructure, and electrolyzer manufacturing.
Green hydrogen helps in decarbonizing industries and reducing fossil fuel imports.
mainsPoints:
Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate change mitigation goals.
Analyze the key challenges and opportunities in establishing a robust green hydrogen ecosystem in India, including technological, financial, and infrastructural aspects.
Evaluate the potential impact of the National Green Hydrogen Mission on various industrial sectors and India's position in the global energy market.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic image of a green hydrogen production facility.
articles.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, such as solar or wind power.

articles.headline: Government Announces New Defense Procurement Policy to Boost Indigenization and 'Make in India'
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced a new Defense Procurement Policy (DPP) aimed at significantly boosting indigenization and promoting the 'Make in India' initiative in the defense sector.
• The policy introduces streamlined procedures, enhanced financial incentives, and a greater emphasis on domestic research and development (R&D) and manufacturing.
• It prioritizes procurement from Indian companies, particularly Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSMEs), to foster a self-reliant defense industrial base.
• The new policy seeks to reduce India's reliance on foreign defense imports and create a robust ecosystem for defense production.
• Key provisions include faster acquisition cycles, enhanced trial procedures, and a focus on cutting-edge technologies developed domestically.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to Internal Security through Communication Networks
Role of External State and Non-state Actors in creating challenges to internal security
Security Challenges and their Management in Border Areas
prelimsPoints: New Defense Procurement Policy (DPP) announced to boost 'Make in India' in defense.
Prioritizes procurement from Indian companies and MSMEs.
Aims to reduce foreign defense imports and enhance R&D.
mainsPoints:
Analyze the significance of indigenization in the defense sector for India's national security and economic growth.
Discuss the key features of the new Defense Procurement Policy and evaluate its potential to transform India's defense industrial base.
Examine the challenges in achieving self-reliance in defense manufacturing, including technological gaps, funding, and private sector participation.
articles.imageDescription: A 'Make in India' logo with military equipment in the background.
articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest importers of defense equipment, making indigenization a critical strategic goal.

highlights: Kerala becomes the first Indian state to officially eradicate extreme poverty, a significant milestone in social welfare and governance.
ISRO successfully launches CMS-03, its heaviest communications satellite, bolstering India's space communication capabilities.
India's Women's Cricket Team creates history by winning its maiden Women's Cricket World Cup title in 2025.
A series of fatal road and rail accidents across Jodhpur, Jaipur, Chevella, Chhattisgarh, and Mirzapur raise serious concerns about national safety standards.
Andhra Pradesh court sentences five individuals to death for the 2015 assassination of Chittoor Mayor and her husband, asserting judicial resolve against political violence.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's recent achievements, consider the following statements:
1. Kerala is the first state in India to eradicate extreme poverty.
2. ISRO's CMS-03 is the heaviest communication satellite launched by India till date.
3. The Indian Women's Cricket Team won its first Women's Cricket World Cup in 2025.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct based on the news for October 31, 2025. Kerala achieved the unique distinction of eradicating extreme poverty. ISRO successfully launched CMS-03, which is its heaviest communications satellite. The Indian Women's Cricket Team secured its first Women's Cricket World Cup title.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'H files' press conference, recently in news, is associated with which of the following?
options: (a) Allegations of electoral fraud in the 2024 Haryana Legislative Assembly election.
(b) Unveiling of a new health policy for remote areas of Himachal Pradesh.
(c) Disclosure of classified historical documents related to Hyderabad's accession.
(d) Launch of a new housing scheme for economically weaker sections in Haryana.
prelimsMocks.answer: (a) Allegations of electoral fraud in the 2024 Haryana Legislative Assembly election.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Rahul Gandhi conducted a press conference titled 'H files' to make allegations about massive electoral fraud in the 2024 Haryana Legislative Assembly election, specifically concerning voter list manipulation and EVM tampering.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in India:
1. Article 44 of the Indian Constitution mandates the state to endeavor to secure a UCC for the citizens.
2. Goa is the only state in India that currently has a Uniform Civil Code.
3. The Supreme Court has recently given a definitive ruling on the implementation of UCC across India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as UCC is a Directive Principle of State Policy under Article 44. Statement 2 is correct, Goa has a UCC. Statement 3 is incorrect; the Supreme Court is still deliberating on petitions related to UCC and has not given a definitive ruling on its nationwide implementation as of October 31, 2025.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is primarily responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: While the RBI is the central bank, the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is the specific body within the RBI framework that is entrusted with the task of fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target.

prelimsMocks.question: The term 'Green Hydrogen Mission', recently in news, is related to:
options: (a) A mission to plant 1 billion trees across India by 2030.
(b) A national initiative to produce hydrogen using renewable energy sources.
(c) A project to clean major rivers using advanced green technologies.
(d) A program for sustainable agriculture practices and organic farming.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) A national initiative to produce hydrogen using renewable energy sources.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export. Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water using renewable electricity, making it a clean fuel source.

mainsMocks.question: The eradication of extreme poverty in Kerala highlights the success of a targeted, multi-dimensional approach to social welfare. Discuss the key features of the 'Kerala Model' that contributed to this achievement and analyze its potential replicability in other Indian states.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Welfare Schemes for Vulnerable Sections, Governance; GS1: Social Empowerment
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce Kerala's achievement as a benchmark. Detail the key features of the 'Kerala Model' (decentralized planning, strong public services in health and education, social security, land reforms, gender equality initiatives). Analyze how these factors collectively addressed extreme poverty. Discuss challenges and opportunities for replication in other states (different socio-economic contexts, political will, administrative capacity, resource availability).

mainsMocks.question: India's burgeoning digital public infrastructure (DPI) has been hailed as a model for inclusive growth and efficient governance. Critically analyze the benefits and potential risks associated with India's DPI, especially concerning data privacy and cybersecurity.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: E-governance, Government policies and interventions; GS3: Science and Technology, Cybersecurity
mainsMocks.approach: Define DPI with examples like Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker. Discuss benefits: financial inclusion, ease of service delivery, economic growth, transparency. Critically analyze risks: data privacy breaches, surveillance concerns, digital divide, cybersecurity vulnerabilities, exclusion of those without digital access. Suggest measures to mitigate risks and ensure inclusive, secure DPI.

mainsMocks.question: Despite significant efforts, road and rail accidents continue to be a major public safety concern in India. Analyze the underlying causes of these frequent accidents and suggest comprehensive policy measures, technological interventions, and behavioral changes required to enhance safety standards.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Disaster and Disaster Management; GS3: Infrastructure (Roads, Railways), Public Safety
mainsMocks.approach: Acknowledge the gravity of recent accidents. Identify underlying causes: poor infrastructure, driver negligence, lack of enforcement, inadequate emergency response, human error, railway crossing issues, outdated signaling systems. Propose policy measures (strict enforcement, better regulations, inter-state coordination), technological interventions (AI in traffic management, advanced signaling, collision avoidance systems), and behavioral changes (awareness campaigns, driver training, pedestrian safety).

quickBytes.text: Kerala is the first state in India to eradicate extreme poverty.
CMS-03 is ISRO's heaviest communications satellite.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is responsible for fixing India's benchmark interest rate.
Article 44 of the Indian Constitution pertains to the Uniform Civil Code (UCC).
Goa is the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production.
Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) refers to foundational digital systems like Aadhaar and UPI.
Churachandpur, where UKNA militants were killed, is a district in Manipur.
Ramdarash Mishra was a prominent Hindi poet and writer.
India is one of the world's largest importers of defense equipment.

GS1.headline: Historic Triumph: India Clinches Maiden Women's Cricket World Cup Title 2025
GS1.explainer:
• The Indian Women's Cricket Team made history by winning their first-ever Women's Cricket World Cup title in 2025.
• This victory marks a significant milestone for women's sports in India, boosting morale and inspiring a new generation of athletes.
• The team's consistent performance throughout the tournament culminated in a thrilling final match, showcasing exceptional talent and teamwork.
• The win is expected to further popularize women's cricket and lead to increased investment and recognition for female athletes in the country.
• Celebrations erupted nationwide as fans lauded the team's groundbreaking achievement.
prelimsPoints: India won its first Women's Cricket World Cup in 2025.
This is a significant achievement for women's sports in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the impact of such sporting achievements on promoting gender equality and women's empowerment in India.
Analyze the role of sports in fostering national identity and unity, using the example of India's World Cup win.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for professional women's sports in India, particularly in terms of infrastructure and funding.
GS1.imageDescription: Indian Women's Cricket team celebrating their World Cup victory.
GS1.trivia: The first Women's Cricket World Cup was held in England in 1973, two years before the men's inaugural event.

GS1.headline: Nation Gripped by Multiple Fatal Road and Rail Accidents Across Jodhpur, Jaipur, Chevella, Chhattisgarh, and Mirzapur
GS1.explainer:
• A series of tragic accidents occurred across India, including a tempo traveller crashing into a parked truck in Jodhpur, killing 15.
• In Jaipur, 19 people died and 40 were injured when a speeding dump truck collided with 17 vehicles, causing a massive pile-up.
• Near Chevella, Telangana, 24 people were killed and 24 injured when a truck collided with an RTC bus.
• A passenger train collided with a cargo train near Bilaspur, Chhattisgarh, resulting in 11 fatalities in what's termed the '2025 Chhattisgarh train collision'.
• Six people were killed after being hit by the Kalka-Howrah Express while crossing railway tracks in Mirzapur, Uttar Pradesh.
• These incidents highlight critical issues in road and rail safety, infrastructure, and emergency response mechanisms in India.
prelimsPoints: Major fatal road accidents reported in Jodhpur (15 killed), Jaipur (19 killed), and Chevella (24 killed).
A passenger-cargo train collision in Bilaspur, Chhattisgarh, killed 11.
Six people died after being hit by a train in Mirzapur, UP.
mainsPoints: Analyze the major causes of road and rail accidents in India and propose comprehensive measures for enhancing safety standards.
Discuss the role of technology, infrastructure development, and public awareness campaigns in mitigating accident risks.
Evaluate the effectiveness of India's disaster management framework in responding to mass casualty events and suggest improvements.
GS1.imageDescription: Rescue workers at the site of a train collision.
GS1.trivia: India accounts for about 11% of the total number of road accident deaths in the world.

GS1.headline: At Least Nine Killed in Stampede at Venkateswara Temple in Srikakulam
GS1.explainer:
• A tragic stampede at the Venkateswara Temple in Srikakulam, Andhra Pradesh, resulted in the deaths of at least nine people.
• The incident occurred during a major religious festival, where a large crowd had gathered, leading to overcrowding and a crush.
• Several others were injured and received immediate medical attention, with authorities launching an inquiry into the cause of the stampede.
• This event highlights the persistent challenges in crowd management and safety protocols at large religious gatherings in India.
• State authorities have announced ex-gratia payments for the victims' families and initiated steps to prevent recurrence.
prelimsPoints: A stampede at Venkateswara Temple in Srikakulam, Andhra Pradesh, killed at least nine people.
The incident occurred during a religious festival.
mainsPoints:
Discuss the recurring issue of stampedes at religious sites in India and analyze the systemic failures in crowd management.
Suggest comprehensive strategies for enhancing public safety and disaster preparedness at large gatherings, especially religious festivals.
Examine the role of local administration, temple trusts, and technology in preventing such tragedies.
GS1.imageDescription: Emergency services at the scene of a stampede.
GS1.trivia: Major stampedes in India often occur during festivals like Kumbh Mela or at popular pilgrimage sites due to massive crowds.

GS1.headline: Noted Hindi Poet and Writer Ramdarash Mishra Passes Away at 101
GS1.explainer:
• Renowned Hindi poet and writer Ramdarash Mishra passed away at the age of 101, leaving behind a rich legacy in Indian literature.
• Mishra was celebrated for his profound contributions to Hindi poetry, fiction, and literary criticism, spanning several decades.
• His works often reflected the rural Indian landscape, human emotions, and social realities, resonating deeply with readers.
• He was a recipient of numerous prestigious literary awards, recognizing his significant impact on contemporary Hindi literature.
• His demise marks the end of an era for Hindi literary circles, with tributes pouring in from across the country.
gsSubTopics: Indian Culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: Ramdarash Mishra, a noted Hindi poet and writer, passed away at 101.
He was known for his contributions to Hindi literature and received various awards.
GS1.trivia: Ramdarash Mishra was born in Dumri village, Gorakhpur district, Uttar Pradesh.

GS2.headline: Kerala Declared First Indian State to Eradicate Extreme Poverty, Setting National Benchmark
GS2.explainer:
• Kerala has officially achieved the eradication of extreme poverty, making it the first state in India to reach this milestone.
• The achievement is attributed to robust social welfare schemes, targeted interventions, and effective implementation of poverty alleviation programs.
• This success is a result of years of concerted efforts focusing on providing basic necessities, employment opportunities, and social security nets to vulnerable populations.
• The 'Kerala Model' of development, emphasizing public services and grassroots planning, is being studied as a potential blueprint for other states.
• The initiative included identifying and supporting families below the extreme poverty line through various government and local self-government projects.
prelimsPoints: Kerala is the first Indian state to eradicate extreme poverty.
The achievement is based on a multi-dimensional approach to poverty.
The 'Kerala Model' is often cited for its focus on social development indicators.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key components of the 'Kerala Model' that contributed to extreme poverty eradication.
Analyze the role of decentralized governance and social welfare schemes in achieving this milestone.
Examine the potential replicability of Kerala's success in other Indian states, considering socio-economic diversities.
GS2.imageDescription: A map of Kerala highlighting its achievement in poverty eradication.
GS2.trivia: Kerala boasts one of the highest Human Development Index (HDI) scores among Indian states, often compared to some developed nations.

GS2.headline: Andhra Pradesh Court Sentences Five to Death for 2015 Chittoor Mayor Assassination
GS2.explainer:
• A special court in Andhra Pradesh has sentenced five individuals to death for the assassination of Chittoor Mayor Katari Anuradha and her husband Katari Mohan in November 2015.
• The verdict comes after nearly a decade of trial, underscoring the judicial system's commitment to delivering justice in high-profile criminal cases.
• The assassination, believed to be politically motivated, had sent shockwaves across the state and raised concerns about political violence.
• The court's decision is seen as a strong message against political vigilantism and criminal elements in public life.
• The case involved extensive investigation and prosecution, highlighting the complexities of bringing perpetrators of such crimes to justice.
gsSubTopics: Structure, Organization and Functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
prelimsPoints: Five people sentenced to death for the 2015 Chittoor Mayor assassination.
The verdict was delivered by a court in Andhra Pradesh.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of the judiciary in curbing political violence and ensuring accountability in high-profile criminal cases.
Analyze the debates surrounding capital punishment in India, in light of such verdicts.
Examine the challenges faced by the Indian criminal justice system in ensuring speedy trials for complex and politically sensitive cases.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel and court files, symbolizing the judicial process.
GS2.trivia: The Indian Penal Code (IPC) specifies various offenses for which capital punishment can be awarded, including murder.

GS2.headline: Rahul Gandhi Alleges Massive Electoral Fraud in 'H Files' Press Conference
GS2.explainer:
• Congress leader Rahul Gandhi conducted a press conference titled 'H files,' making serious allegations of massive electoral fraud in the 2024 Haryana Legislative Assembly election.
• Gandhi presented what he claimed to be evidence of irregularities, including manipulation of voter lists, electronic voting machine (EVM) tampering, and undue influence.
• The allegations have sparked a political controversy, with demands for an independent inquiry into the electoral process in Haryana.
• The ruling party has vehemently denied the accusations, calling them baseless and an attempt to discredit democratic institutions.
• This incident reignites the debate on electoral reforms, transparency, and the integrity of the election process in India.
prelimsPoints: Rahul Gandhi made allegations of electoral fraud in the 2024 Haryana Legislative Assembly election.
The allegations were made during a press conference titled 'H files'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges to the integrity of electoral processes in India and the measures needed to enhance transparency and public trust.
Analyze the role of the Election Commission of India in addressing allegations of electoral malpractices and ensuring free and fair elections.
Examine the arguments for and against the use of EVMs in Indian elections, considering recent controversies.
GS2.imageDescription: Rahul Gandhi addressing a press conference with 'H files' banner.
GS2.trivia: The first general election in independent India was held from October 1951 to February 1952.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Continues Deliberations on Petitions Related to Uniform Civil Code
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India continued its deliberations on a batch of petitions challenging and supporting the implementation of a Uniform Civil Code (UCC).
• The court is examining various aspects, including constitutional validity, implications for personal laws, and the impact on religious and cultural diversity.
• The debate surrounding UCC involves fundamental rights, secularism, and the state's power to legislate on personal matters.
• Various stakeholders, including religious bodies, legal experts, and women's rights organizations, are presenting their arguments before the court.
• A potential ruling or guidance from the Supreme Court could have far-reaching consequences for family laws across different communities in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—Historical Underpinnings, Evolution, Features, Amendments, Significant Provisions and Basic Structure
Functions and Responsibilities of the Union and the States
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court is deliberating on petitions related to the Uniform Civil Code (UCC).
UCC is mentioned under Article 44 of the Indian Constitution (DPSP).
It aims to replace diverse personal laws with a common set of laws governing marriage, divorce, inheritance, etc.
mainsPoints:
Critically analyze the arguments for and against the implementation of a Uniform Civil Code in India, considering its constitutional provisions and socio-cultural implications.
Discuss how a UCC could impact gender justice and religious freedom, drawing upon various perspectives.
Examine the role of the judiciary in interpreting and guiding the implementation of sensitive policy matters like the UCC.
GS2.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building.
GS2.trivia: Goa is the only Indian state currently with a Uniform Civil Code, inherited from its Portuguese civil law tradition.

GS2.headline: India-Africa Economic Forum Concludes, Pledges Enhanced Cooperation and New Partnerships
GS2.explainer:
• The India-Africa Economic Forum concluded with commitments for enhanced cooperation across various sectors, including trade, investment, technology transfer, and capacity building.
• The forum emphasized strengthening South-South cooperation and fostering sustainable development partnerships between India and African nations.
• Several new agreements and memoranda of understanding (MoUs) were signed, particularly in areas of renewable energy, digital infrastructure, and healthcare.
• Leaders highlighted the shared vision for a multipolar world order and mutual growth, moving beyond traditional donor-recipient dynamics.
• The forum underscored India's growing strategic and economic engagement with the African continent.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, Regional and Global Groupings and Agreements involving India
Effect of Policies and Politics of Developed and Developing Countries on India’s Interests
prelimsPoints: The India-Africa Economic Forum focused on strengthening trade, investment, and technology transfer.
New agreements were signed in sectors like renewable energy and digital infrastructure.
It emphasizes South-South cooperation.
mainsPoints:
Analyze the strategic significance of India's engagement with African nations in the context of global geopolitics and economic competition.
Discuss the key pillars of India's foreign policy towards Africa and evaluate its effectiveness in fostering mutual growth and development.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in expanding India-Africa cooperation, particularly in areas of infrastructure and resource development.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and African flags displayed at a conference venue.
GS2.trivia: India is one of the largest trading partners of Africa, with trade volumes continuously increasing over the past two decades.

GS2.headline: Government Unveils New National Policy on Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI)
GS2.explainer:
• The Indian government unveiled a new comprehensive National Policy on Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI), aimed at accelerating digital transformation.
• The policy focuses on expanding access to digital services, promoting innovation, and ensuring data privacy and security for citizens.
• It outlines a framework for the development and governance of foundational digital systems like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, and their integration.
• The DPI policy is expected to further boost India's digital economy and enhance the delivery of public services, fostering financial inclusion and economic empowerment.
• Emphasis is placed on creating an open, secure, and interoperable digital ecosystem, leveraging India's success in digital payments and identity.
gsSubTopics: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Government launched a new National Policy on Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
DPI includes systems like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
The policy aims to expand digital services, promote innovation, and ensure data security.
mainsPoints:
Discuss the significance of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in fostering inclusive growth and efficient governance in India.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a robust DPI framework, including data privacy concerns, digital divide, and cybersecurity threats.
Evaluate how India's DPI model can serve as a blueprint for other developing nations seeking digital transformation.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting interconnected digital services and platforms.
GS2.trivia: India's Unified Payments Interface (UPI) has become a global benchmark for real-time digital payments, processing billions of transactions monthly.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches CMS-03, India's Heaviest Communications Satellite
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched CMS-03, its heaviest communications satellite to date, from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre in Andhra Pradesh.
• CMS-03 is designed to provide advanced telecommunication services, including high-speed internet, broadcasting, and disaster management support across India and its surrounding regions.
• The satellite's enhanced capacity and extended lifespan are expected to significantly boost India's communication infrastructure.
• This launch marks another major milestone for ISRO, reaffirming India's growing capabilities in space technology and applications.
• The mission utilized ISRO's Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle (GSLV) or a similar heavy-lift launcher.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology
prelimsPoints: CMS-03 is ISRO's heaviest communications satellite.
It was launched from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
It aims to enhance telecommunication services.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of advanced communication satellites like CMS-03 for India's digital infrastructure and national security.
Analyze ISRO's role in leveraging space technology for socio-economic development in India.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India's space program in the coming decade, particularly in heavy-lift capabilities.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of the CMS-03 satellite orbiting Earth.
GS3.trivia: India's first experimental satellite, Aryabhata, was launched by the Soviet Union in 1975.

GS3.headline: Four United Kuki National Army Militants Killed in Manipur Encounter
GS3.explainer:
• Four militants belonging to the United Kuki National Army (UKNA) were killed in an encounter with security forces in Churachandpur, Manipur.
• The operation was part of ongoing efforts by security forces to counter insurgency and maintain peace and stability in the region.
• The encounter highlights the persistent security challenges and ethnic tensions in certain parts of Manipur.
• Weapons and incriminating documents were recovered from the site of the encounter, suggesting planned militant activities.
• This incident underscores the complex internal security scenario in India's North-Eastern states.
gsSubTopics: Security Challenges and their Management in Border Areas
Linkages of Organized Crime with Terrorism
Various Security Forces and Agencies and their Mandate
prelimsPoints: Four UKNA militants were killed in an encounter in Churachandpur, Manipur.
The incident is related to ongoing efforts against insurgency in the North-East.
mainsPoints:
Analyze the root causes of insurgency in Manipur and the broader North-Eastern region of India.
Discuss the strategies adopted by the Indian government and security forces to counter militancy and bring peace to the region.
Examine the role of ethnic dynamics and cross-border influences in perpetuating security challenges in the North-East.
GS3.imageDescription: Indian security forces patrolling a forested area in Manipur.
GS3.trivia: Manipur shares a border with Myanmar, which often contributes to cross-border insurgency activities.

GS3.headline: RBI's Monetary Policy Committee Concludes Meeting, Focus on Inflation and Growth Outlook
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its latest meeting, with key decisions expected on interest rates and liquidity measures.
• The primary focus of the MPC remains on managing inflation within the target band while supporting economic growth amidst global uncertainties.
• Experts anticipate the RBI to maintain a cautious stance, potentially holding the repo rate steady or making marginal adjustments based on current economic indicators.
• The committee's forward guidance will be crucial for market stability and investor sentiment, particularly regarding future inflation trajectory and growth projections.
• Discussions likely included global economic headwinds, domestic demand, credit growth, and the impact of monsoon on food inflation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources, Growth, Development and Employment
prelimsPoints: RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) met to review economic conditions.
Key focus areas are inflation management and economic growth.
MPC decisions influence interest rates and liquidity in the economy.
mainsPoints:
Evaluate the effectiveness of RBI's monetary policy tools in managing inflation and supporting economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Discuss the challenges faced by the MPC in balancing inflation control with the imperative for economic recovery.
Analyze the interplay between fiscal policy and monetary policy in achieving macroeconomic stability in India.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI logo and graphs depicting economic indicators.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.

GS3.headline: Air Quality Deteriorates in Northern India, Raising Public Health Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• Air quality across major cities in Northern India, including Delhi-NCR, has significantly deteriorated, entering 'very poor' and 'severe' categories.
• The worsening air quality is attributed to a combination of meteorological factors, stubble burning in neighboring states, vehicular emissions, and industrial pollution.
• Public health advisories have been issued, urging residents to take precautions, especially vulnerable groups like children and the elderly.
• This annual phenomenon highlights the urgent need for long-term, coordinated strategies to combat air pollution across state boundaries.
• Discussions are ongoing regarding emergency measures such as odd-even schemes, construction bans, and restrictions on polluting industries.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, Environmental Pollution and Degradation, Environmental Impact Assessment
prelimsPoints: Air quality in Northern India, especially Delhi-NCR, deteriorated to 'very poor'/'severe' categories.
Causes include stubble burning, vehicular emissions, and industrial pollution.
AQI measures the level of air pollution.
mainsPoints:
Analyze the multi-faceted causes of air pollution in Northern India and critically evaluate the effectiveness of current policy interventions.
Discuss the socio-economic and health impacts of deteriorating air quality and suggest comprehensive, long-term solutions involving inter-state cooperation.
Examine the role of technology, public awareness, and behavioral changes in mitigating air pollution.
GS3.imageDescription: A hazy skyline of Delhi, indicating severe air pollution.
GS3.trivia: PM2.5 refers to atmospheric particulate matter that has a diameter of less than 2.5 micrometers, which is about 3% the diameter of a human hair.

GS3.headline: Latest Phase of National Green Hydrogen Mission Launched, Boosting Renewable Energy Goals
GS3.explainer:
• The government launched the latest phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, intensifying efforts to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
• This phase focuses on scaling up production capacities, developing green hydrogen export infrastructure, and fostering domestic manufacturing of electrolyzers.
• The mission aims to reduce India's reliance on fossil fuel imports and contribute significantly to its climate change commitments and energy security.
• Financial incentives, R&D support, and policy frameworks are being strengthened to attract investments and accelerate technological advancements in the sector.
• Green hydrogen is seen as a critical component in decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors like steel, refining, and fertilizers.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, Environmental Pollution and Degradation, Environmental Impact Assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Latest phase of National Green Hydrogen Mission launched.
Aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen.
Focuses on scaling production, export infrastructure, and electrolyzer manufacturing.
Green hydrogen helps in decarbonizing industries and reducing fossil fuel imports.
mainsPoints:
Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate change mitigation goals.
Analyze the key challenges and opportunities in establishing a robust green hydrogen ecosystem in India, including technological, financial, and infrastructural aspects.
Evaluate the potential impact of the National Green Hydrogen Mission on various industrial sectors and India's position in the global energy market.
GS3.imageDescription: A futuristic image of a green hydrogen production facility.
GS3.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, such as solar or wind power.

GS3.headline: Government Announces New Defense Procurement Policy to Boost Indigenization and 'Make in India'
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced a new Defense Procurement Policy (DPP) aimed at significantly boosting indigenization and promoting the 'Make in India' initiative in the defense sector.
• The policy introduces streamlined procedures, enhanced financial incentives, and a greater emphasis on domestic research and development (R&D) and manufacturing.
• It prioritizes procurement from Indian companies, particularly Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSMEs), to foster a self-reliant defense industrial base.
• The new policy seeks to reduce India's reliance on foreign defense imports and create a robust ecosystem for defense production.
• Key provisions include faster acquisition cycles, enhanced trial procedures, and a focus on cutting-edge technologies developed domestically.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to Internal Security through Communication Networks
Role of External State and Non-state Actors in creating challenges to internal security
Security Challenges and their Management in Border Areas
prelimsPoints: New Defense Procurement Policy (DPP) announced to boost 'Make in India' in defense.
Prioritizes procurement from Indian companies and MSMEs.
Aims to reduce foreign defense imports and enhance R&D.
mainsPoints:
Analyze the significance of indigenization in the defense sector for India's national security and economic growth.
Discuss the key features of the new Defense Procurement Policy and evaluate its potential to transform India's defense industrial base.
Examine the challenges in achieving self-reliance in defense manufacturing, including technological gaps, funding, and private sector participation.
GS3.imageDescription: A 'Make in India' logo with military equipment in the background.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the world's largest importers of defense equipment, making indigenization a critical strategic goal.
